title: HTTP Semantics, Messages, Versions, and the Wire Model · Language Lens: Intermediary Transformation
summary: A raw implementation copies endpoint behavior into its parser, confuses resources with representations, infers message bodies from method names, and treats HTTP/1.1 syntax as universal HTTP semantics. Planned artifact: Add and verify the intermediary transformation increment in version-neutral message and semantics model. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Trace origin, proxy, gateway, tunnel, cache, and user-agent transformations while retaining end-to-end and hop evidence.
Interpret HTTP version as capability and conformance information while rejecting unsupported major versions and unsafe downgrade assumptions.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Predict Intermediary Transformation · Theory Intermediary Transformation
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory protocol team handles case 9761 in a privacy-sensitive proxy translating between two parser implementations. Build deterministic Go evidence to derive message content from method and status semantics before wire framing; preserve this operating constraint: the browser model cannot open a socket or claim packet-level proof; then block extra QPACK streams and fall back without removing HTTP/2 availability. Browser code models bounded byte and state decisions only. Sockets, listeners, live TLS, proxies, packet capture, fuzz campaigns, races, load, HTTP/2, HTTP/3, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Intermediary Transformation contract for the supplied data.

Evidence target: Predict Intermediary Transformation · Theory Intermediary Transformation.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for trace origin, proxy, gateway, tunnel, cache, and user-agent transformations while retaining end-to-end and hop evidence. For Predict Intermediary Transformation · Theory Intermediary Transformation, keep Intermediary Transformation connected to executable Go behavior evidence.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Declare types, ownership, goroutine lifetime, failure contract, and observable result; trace value copies, aliases, blocking, and happens-before edges; run the smallest isolated program; then change one boundary input and retain a regression invariant. steps.title: Build Intermediary Transformation evidence · Theory Intermediary Transformation
steps.instruction:
Append a compile-ready pure-Go function headed "// Evidence: httpproto-intermediary-transformation-1jk2tu" that uses case 4666 to derive message content from method and status semantics before wire framing. Return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not open sockets or listeners, negotiate TLS, contact networks or proxies, capture packets, read host state, or use credentials; verify those boundaries later with the named Go 1.26 protocol transfer gates.
